hero.title2: A hormone test can finally explain
hero.desc: When your energy drops, periods change, weight shifts, or your mood feels "not you," it's exhausting to keep guessing. At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, we use doctor-led, blood-test guided checks to find the pattern, then map out the next steps clearly.
hero.address: LG 10, Lower Ground Floor, Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, 50450 Kuala Lumpur

whatIs.title: What Is a Hormone Test?
whatIs.desc: A hormone test is usually a blood test that measures specific hormones in your body. Hormones act like messages. They influence energy, sleep, metabolism, periods, fertility, sex drive, skin, and mood.
whatIs.note: Some services also offer saliva hormone profiles, but for many medical decisions, blood testing is the standard starting point, especially when symptoms are strong or treatment decisions are being considered.

reasons.title: Why People Book a Hormone Test
reasons.w2: PCOS concerns (acne, unwanted hair, stubborn weight gain)
reasons.w4: Perimenopause symptoms, sleep issues, hot flushes
reasons.w5: Hair thinning, skin changes, mood shifts
reasons.m1: Low libido, erectile dysfunction, low confidence
reasons.m2: Fatigue, brain fog, poor recovery from workouts
reasons.edNote: Nexus Clinic's men's ED and libido pathway specifically mentions symptom assessment plus blood tests, hormone level checks, and cardiovascular evaluation, because ED can be linked to underlying health issues.

tests.title: The Most Common Hormone Tests in Malaysia
tests.subtitle: What They Check and Why It Matters
tests.t1Desc: Thyroid imbalance can show up as fatigue, weight change, hair loss, constipation, anxiety, and menstrual disruption.
tests.t2Sub: FSH, LH, estradiol (E2), progesterone, prolactin, AMH
tests.t2Desc: Pantai Hospital offers fertility screening packages including female hormone profile + transvaginal ultrasound + specialist review.
tests.t3Desc: Often linked to PCOS and acne. A KL women's hormone screening guide specifically links hirsutism and acne to androgen testing.
tests.t4Sub: Total testosterone, free testosterone, SHBG, LH/FSH
tests.t4Desc: Nexus Clinic's TRT page describes testing total serum testosterone plus other safety labs at baseline and during follow-up.
tests.t5Desc: Selected cases only. In Malaysia, "stress profile" panels are marketed but should be chosen carefully based on symptoms and doctor review.

bestTime.title: Best Time to Do a Hormone Test
bestTime.subtitle: Timing Matters More Than People Think
bestTime.womenTimingDesc: FSH, LH, prolactin, and estradiol are often measured around day 3 to 4 of the menstrual cycle, when hormones are closer to baseline.
bestTime.progesteroneDesc: Test mid-luteal, about 7 days before your expected period. ASRM notes progesterone typically peaks 6 to 8 days after ovulation.
bestTime.menTimingDesc: Morning sampling is often preferred for testosterone. Many clinical pathways prefer morning sampling when diagnosing low testosterone.

preparation.title: How to Prepare for a Hormone Blood Test
preparation.subtitle: Preparation depends on which hormones you're testing
preparation.fastingDesc: Many hormone tests do not require fasting, but if your panel includes glucose, insulin, or cholesterol, your clinic may advise fasting. Always follow the clinic's instructions.
preparation.biotinDesc: A KL women's hormone screening guide warns that biotin supplements may interfere with results for tests like thyroid panels. If you take biotin (hair, skin, nails supplements), tell your clinic before testing.
preparation.tip1: Bring your cycle dates (first day of last period, usual cycle length)
preparation.tip3: Avoid heavy exercise right before certain hormone tests (your clinician will guide you)

results.title: What Happens After You Get Results?
results.desc: This is where a lot of people feel lost. A report will show "normal ranges," but hormone ranges change based on:
results.keyQuestion: The right question is not "Is it normal?" It's "Does this result match what I'm feeling, and what should we do next?"
results.nexusApproach: Nexus Clinic's approach across services is to use blood-test guided, doctor monitored programmes, and their women's fertility content describes hormone checks as part of a broader clinical picture.

cost.title: Hormone Test Cost in Malaysia
cost.subtitle: Kuala Lumpur Pricing Reality
cost.intro: Costs vary depending on how many hormones you test and whether imaging or consult is included.
cost.pantaiTitle: Real published price references from Malaysia providers:
cost.pkg1Desc: Fertility screening package for women: RM699 including female hormone profile test, transvaginal ultrasound, specialist consult/review
cost.pkg2Desc: "Comprehensive Blood Profile Female" listed at RM450
cost.pkg3Desc: Advanced hormone blood test listed at RM1,498

market.title: Market Positioning in Malaysia
market.m1Desc: Clinics position "hormone imbalance tests" as symptom-driven screening. Example: Prinz Klinik promotes hormone imbalance testing to identify root causes of symptoms.
market.m2Desc: Hospitals publish transparent test menus and packages. Panti publishes hormone test price lists and fertility screening packages that combine hormones plus ultrasound and specialist review.
market.m3Desc: Wellness clinics sell bigger "advanced hormone" bundles. Rosewood promotes a premium hormone blood test bundle at a higher price point.
market.nexusTitle: How Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur can stand out
market.n1: Make timing clear (cycle day testing, mid-luteal progesterone, morning testosterone)
market.n2: Link symptoms to practical next steps, not just a long list of tests
market.n3: Keep it medical and guided, since Nexus highlights blood-test guided programmes and doctor monitoring

faq.q1: What is a hormone test used for?
faq.a1: A hormone test checks hormone levels to help investigate symptoms like irregular periods, fertility issues, acne and unwanted hair, fatigue, thyroid symptoms, and low libido or ED.
faq.q2: How much does a hormone test cost in Malaysia?
faq.a2: It depends on the panel. Some hospitals publish per-test prices (FSH RM65, LH RM65, estradiol RM95, AMH RM559.70), while fertility packages that include ultrasound and specialist review can be around RM699.
faq.q3: Which hormone tests should women do for irregular periods?
faq.a3: A KL women's hormone screening guide suggests evaluating thyroid (TSH ± Free T4), prolactin, and FSH/LH/estradiol after excluding pregnancy, with additional tests based on symptoms.
faq.q4: Which hormone tests matter for PCOS?
faq.a4: For androgen excess signs like hirsutism or acne, testing may include total testosterone, DHEA-S, and 17-OHP, plus metabolic screening.
faq.q5: When is the best time to test female hormones?
faq.a5: FSH, LH, prolactin, and estradiol are often measured around day 3 to 4 of the cycle.
faq.q6: When should I do a progesterone test to confirm ovulation?
faq.a6: Many labs recommend testing about 7 days before your expected period (day 21 in a 28-day cycle). ASRM notes progesterone peaks 6 to 8 days after ovulation.
faq.q7: Do I need to fast before a hormone blood test?
faq.a7: Many hormone tests do not require fasting, but fasting may be needed if your panel includes glucose or lipid tests. Follow clinic instructions.
faq.q8: Can biotin affect hormone test results?
faq.a8: Yes. A KL women's hormone screening guide notes biotin can interfere with results, especially thyroid tests. Tell your clinic if you take biotin.
faq.q9: What hormones are checked for low libido or ED in men?
faq.a9: Nexus Clinic's ED and libido pathway mentions hormone level checks as part of assessment, and testosterone testing is commonly included in men's hormone evaluation.
faq.q10: Why would my doctor repeat a hormone test?
faq.a10: Hormones fluctuate with cycle day, time of day, stress, and sleep. Repeat testing helps confirm a true pattern, especially for progesterone and testosterone timing.
faq.q11: Can I do a hormone test for fatigue and weight gain?
faq.a11: Yes, but it should be targeted. Thyroid tests are commonly used first, and Nexus Clinic discusses hormone assessment as part of medical weight loss evaluation.
faq.a12: Not always. A KL women's hormone screening guide notes that for women aged 45 and above, menopause is usually diagnosed clinically and routine FSH testing is not needed due to fluctuations.

cta.title: Ready to Understand Your Hormones?
cta.desc: If you're searching for a hormone test in Kuala Lumpur or a hormone blood test in Malaysia, start with the right test, on the right day, for the right reason.
cta.address: LG 10, Lower Ground Floor, Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, 50450 Kuala Lumpur
